methods.description: This method involves assigning unique specimen numbers to skeletal elements collected from different sites and individuals. It ensures organized cataloging and easy reference to specific elements in the study.

steps.description: Assign an alias to each individual collected from the sites, referring to the site by abbreviation and number of individual (e.g., individual EPN I).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign aliases to individuals based on site and individual number

steps.description: Assign specimen numbers to skeletal elements per individual, with isolated elements numbered from 1 to 99, EPN I skeletal elements from 100 to 199, EPN II from 200 to 287, MIC I from 300 to 303, and KLK I from 500 to 528.
steps.searchable_summary: Assign specimen numbers to skeletal elements based on individual and site

methods.description: This method involves comparing the new material with existing specimens from various localities and literature to identify and describe the skeletal elements of giant tortoises.

steps.description: Directly compare the new material with specimens from other Greek localities and international collections.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare new skeletal material with existing specimens from various collections

steps.description: Make comparisons based on literature for giant tortoise material from various regions.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform literature-based comparison of giant tortoise material
